div1.entryFree: Paul, name of the thirteenth apostle: R3 I, 1, 138 (Ff John). I, 2, 36. I, 2, 36 I, 3, 45. III, 4, 78. V, 3, 216. Paul's == Saint Paul's Church, the principal cathedral of London: H4A II, 4, 576. H4B I, 2, 58. R2 I, 2, 30. III, 6, 3. H8 V, 4, 16 (O. Edd. Powles or Poules). Nares: "St. Paul's church was a constant place of resort for business and amusement." Our poet mentions the custom of hiring servants there, with allusion to the proverb: "who goes to Westminster for a wife, to St. Paul's for a man, and to Smithfield for a horse, may meet with a whore, a knave, and a jade."
